findings.finding: Per-commit verification has no belt-and-suspenders test ! -e plugins/cortex-core/hooks/cortex-output-filter.sh mirror-absence check. Phase 4 of pre-commit catches drift but the per-commit gate as written defers the check.
findings.reviewer_angle: Mirror coverage and dual-source enforcement gaps
findings.evidence_quote: for p in claude/hooks/cortex-output-filter.sh claude/hooks/output-filters.conf claude/hooks/cortex-sync-permissions.py claude/hooks/bell.ps1 tests/test_output_filter.sh tests/fixtures/hooks/sync-permissions; do [ -e "$p" ] && { echo "FAIL:$p"; exit 1; }; done

findings.finding: Synthesizer-downgraded from A: trailer-truthfulness audit gap. The verification gate [ $R3 -eq 1 ] checks only trailer presence, not grep truthfulness. Once round-3 grep template is fixed (per A-class objection 1), this collapses to the universal observation that implementers could write any trailer text — true of any plan, not fix-invalidating.
findings.reviewer_angle: Round-3 grep correctness against pre-commit working tree
